explanation: The triple ensemble of t_learner_lgb + t_learner_ridge + hurdle with equal weights achieves the highest holdout lower CI (0.4523) across all 108 combinations evaluated. This represents a substantial 2.2% improvement over the best single model (0.4424). The combination is compelling because: (1) It includes models with very different CV performance profiles - hurdle leads CV (0.4004), t_learner_lgb is mid-tier (0.3620), and t_learner_ridge is weakest (0.1274), suggesting high diversity in their predictions. (2) The models use fundamentally different approaches: hurdle uses a two-stage process, t_learner uses separate treatment/control models, and ridge vs LGB provides algorithmic diversity. (3) Equal weighting outperforms sophisticated weighting schemes (ci_weights achieves only 0.4475), indicating the models complement each other well without needing optimization. (4) The weak CV performance of t_learner_ridge (0.1274) is counterintuitive but likely captures different signal - its inclusion boosts holdout performance, suggesting it identifies a complementary subset of responsive users.
alternatives_considered: t_learner_lgb + hurdle (rank_weights): 0.4510 - Strong performer but adding t_learner_ridge gains +13 basis points
t_learner_lgb + hurdle (equal): 0.4501 - Simpler 2-model ensemble, but loses the diversity benefit of t_learner_ridge
Single hurdle model: ~0.4424 - Best individual model but ensemble adds 99 basis points to lower CI
Larger ensembles with 4+ models: Not in top-20, suggesting diminishing returns and increased noise
overfitting_assessment: Overfitting risk appears LOW. The selected ensemble shows consistent performance across weighting strategies (equal=0.4523, ci_weights=0.4475), indicating robustness. The gain over CV predictions is reasonable - hurdle CV lower CI was 0.3816 vs holdout ensemble 0.4523 (~18% gain), which is substantial but the consistency across strategies and the fact that simpler equal weighting wins suggests this is real signal rather than overfitting. The inclusion of a weak CV performer (t_learner_ridge) that improves holdout results is a positive sign of diversity rather than overfitting - it suggests the ensemble captures complementary patterns. The 2-model alternatives perform nearly as well (0.4501-0.4510), providing validation that the 3-model choice is stable.
